item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Currently there is no restriction on exploring exactly 100,001 rooms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers:

+ show block
– hide block

It's actually the real SCP-184. Not this universe's SCP-184, since the Josef the Foundation found isn't this universe's Joseph, but still the real deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept's been done before, but the journal helped put a good, creepy spin on things that kept me unsettled as I was reading.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The log started out with that heady hubris that shows up before everything goes sideways, and winds up in Horrortown, population Josef. It's as bad as a maze of twisty little passageways, all alike.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That journal at the end makes the whole thing. It's a good repeating-rooms skip, the sort of thing we've seen before, but you successfully put a twist on the twist at the end. Some really freaky stuff!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read the SCP yet, but Submachine is one of my favourite game series of all time, and the sheer SCP atmosphere the movie gives off definitely makes it incredibly fertile material for inclusion/inspiring an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks again! I decided against having exploration logs because I wanted to convey the feeling of emptiness within the rooms. The bunker is infinitely large, so the chances of finding anything are astronomically small. The Foundation was lucky enough to come across Josef's corpse. There are definitely more things in the rooms, but the Foundation just hasn't found most of it. Yet.

The RPS was inspired by the compass from Submachine 3. The entire SCP was inspired by Submachine 3, actually.

Also, the exploration team that Josef found is the one from the disbanded GoI mentioned at the bottom. Or perhaps they were from a different universe's instance of that GoI, that's up to your interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said in chat, I enjoyed this scip. You were inspired by a rather old concept (infinite rooms/topographical glitches) that's been done well before (SCP-184) and you managed to put your own fresh spin on it. There easily could have been a few collapsibles dedicated to exploration logs but you kept the creativity contained in a series of journal logs (which do a good job of creeping me out, personally).

Things I liked about this:

  • Your own invention for the sake of the scip: the Relative Positioning System (RPS)
  • The "not-Josef" situation, which is much deeper than at first glance (infinite Josefs finding infinite not-Josefs)
  • The exploration team found in the maze of rooms: where did they come from? Their existence is intriguing and frankly a bit scary. Definitely captures some of the fear of the unknown horror found in Series I (having a lack of information that is disseminated in just the right way to evoke maximum fear).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've had this one on the back burner for a while, and now it's finally done. Thanks to Djoric FloppyPhoenix, stormbreath, and possibly some others (apologies if I've forgotten about you) for their feedback.
